homepage.heroSubline: Precision irrigation solutions for the Central Valley's unique climate. Serving Elk Grove, Roseville, and Folsom with tailored water management systems.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Horizon Irrigation began as a two-person operation serving Midtown bungalows with outdated sprinkler systems. Today, we design smart irrigation solutions for Sacramento's diverse neighborhoods.
We specialize in drought-resistant designs that account for clay soil expansion and 100°F summers. Our team understands how Sacramento's microclimates affect water distribution.
From historic Land Park homes to Natomas developments, we're committed to water conservation that preserves our region's beauty while adapting to its challenges.

homepage.ctaSubline: Get a free assessment of your system's efficiency before summer peaks

services.name: Drip Irrigation Systems
services.shortDescription: Water-efficient targeted irrigation perfect for Sacramento's drought conditions.
services.description:
Our drip systems deliver water directly to plant roots, reducing evaporation in Sacramento's 100°F summers. We use commercial-grade tubing that withstands clay soil expansion and contraction.

Perfect for native plant gardens in East Sacramento or vegetable beds in Elk Grove backyards. Includes moisture sensors that adjust watering based on real-time conditions - crucial during our unpredictable dry spells.

We retrofit existing systems or design new installations with Sacramento's water restrictions in mind. All work includes training on seasonal adjustments for our Mediterranean climate.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation of soil type and plant water needs
Step 2: Custom design accounting for sun exposure and slope
Step 3: Professional installation with freeze-resistant components
Step 4: Smart controller programming for Sacramento's watering schedules
benefits: 60% less water than traditional sprinklers
Prevents overspray on Sacramento's narrow lots
Reduces weed growth in planting beds
Compliant with California's Tier 2 water restrictions

faq.question: How does drip irrigation handle our clay soil?
faq.answer: We use pressure-compensating emitters and sand filters to prevent clogging from Sacramento's mineral-rich water and soil particles.

faq.question: Can I automate my system?
faq.answer: Yes, we install smart controllers that adjust for Sacramento's weather patterns and mandatory watering days.

faq.question: Do you service older systems?
faq.answer: We specialize in updating 1970s-90s systems common in Land Park and Curtis Park neighborhoods.

services.name: Sprinkler System Repair
services.shortDescription: Fast fixes for broken heads, leaks, and controller issues.
services.description:
When Sacramento's extreme temperatures cause sprinkler components to fail, we provide same-week repairs to prevent water waste. Common issues we address:

• Cracked heads from foot traffic or mowing in suburban yards
• Leaking valves worsened by our mineral-heavy water
• Controller malfunctions during power surges from summer storms

We stock parts compatible with major brands installed in local homes, from older Rain Bird systems in Midtown to newer Hunter models in Natomas developments. Emergency service available when temperatures spike above 100°F to protect your landscaping investment.

All repairs include a water audit to identify other efficiency opportunities specific to your neighborhood's watering restrictions.
process: Step 1: Diagnostic test of water pressure and electrical components
Step 2: Identification of all failure points
Step 3: Repair with upgraded components where needed
Step 4: System test and water usage report
benefits: Prevents costly water bills from leaks
Extends lifespan of existing equipment
Maintains curb appeal during selling season
24/7 emergency service during heat waves

faq.question: Why do my sprinklers keep breaking?
faq.answer: Sacramento's hard water causes mineral buildup, and clay soil shifts can misalign heads - we install durable components to mitigate these issues.

faq.question: How quickly can you respond to leaks?
faq.answer: Priority slots for active leaks, typically within 48 hours in our service areas.

faq.question: Do you repair old galvanized pipe systems?
faq.answer: Yes, we specialize in updating these while minimizing landscape disruption - common in 1960s Arden-Arcade homes.

services.name: Smart Irrigation Controllers
services.shortDescription: Weather-responsive technology that saves water and money.
services.description:
Upgrade to a smart controller that automatically adjusts watering based on Sacramento's microclimates. Our most popular models:

• Rachio 3: Ideal for tech-savvy homeowners in newer Elk Grove developments
• RainMachine: Perfect for larger lots in Folsom with multiple zones

We program each system with local weather data and Sacramento County's watering restrictions already configured. Receive alerts when systems malfunction during our summer thunderstorms or when water pressure drops.

Includes smartphone control so you can adjust settings when away from home - especially useful for snowbirds who winter elsewhere but need to maintain Sacramento properties. Compatible with existing systems in homes of any age.
process: Step 1: Assessment of current wiring and zone configuration
Step 2: Selection of optimal controller for your property size
Step 3: Professional installation with WiFi setup
Step 4: Personalized training on mobile app features
benefits: Automatically skips watering after Folsom rain
Saves 15-30% on summer water bills
Prevents fines for watering on wrong days
Remote monitoring from any location

faq.question: Will this work with my 1980s irrigation system?
faq.answer: Yes, we adapt new controllers to work with older wiring found in Carmichael and Orangevale homes.

faq.question: How does it handle Sacramento's sudden temperature swings?
faq.answer: Controllers use hyperlocal weather data to increase watering before heat waves and reduce during cool spells.

faq.question: What if my WiFi goes down?
faq.answer: Systems continue working on pre-set schedules until connectivity resumes.

services.name: Complete System Installations
services.shortDescription: Custom-designed irrigation for new construction or landscape renovations.
services.description:
From zero-lot-line Midtown homes to sprawling Roseville backyards, we engineer irrigation systems that match your landscape's unique needs. Our process:

1. Detailed analysis of soil composition (especially crucial for Sacramento's clay-heavy areas)
2. Zone mapping based on plant water requirements and sun exposure
3. Installation of commercial-grade components that withstand our climate

We coordinate with landscape architects on new builds throughout the region, ensuring proper grading and drainage to prevent pooling during winter rains. For historic district homes, we design discreet systems that preserve architectural integrity while meeting modern efficiency standards.

Every installation includes a water conservation plan tailored to your neighborhood's specific restrictions and typical weather patterns.
process: Step 1: Topographic survey and soil testing
Step 2: Custom design meeting with 3D visualization
Step 3: Trenching with minimal landscape disruption
Step 4: Final inspection and water audit
benefits: Increase property value with professional-grade system
Proper coverage for Sacramento's varied microclimates
Future-ready design for drought conditions
Compliance with all local water regulations

faq.question: How long does a full installation take?
faq.answer: Typically 2-4 days depending on yard size - we coordinate with Sacramento's dry season for optimal timing.

faq.question: Do you handle permits?
faq.answer: Yes, we manage all paperwork required by Sacramento County and local municipalities.

faq.question: Can you work around mature trees?
faq.answer: We use air-tool trenching to protect roots in established Land Park and East Sac neighborhoods.

services.name: Seasonal System Tune-Ups
services.shortDescription: Twice-yearly service to optimize efficiency for Sacramento's climate swings.
services.description:
Our most popular maintenance program prepares your system for Sacramento's distinct seasons:

Spring Activation:
• Winter damage inspection from our occasional freezes
• Nozzle cleaning for mineral deposits from hard water
• Schedule programming for summer restrictions

Fall Winterization:
• Complete blow-out to prevent pipe bursts
• Controller reprogramming for reduced watering
• Valve insulation for frost protection

Particularly critical for vacation homes in Granite Bay or snowbird properties that sit empty during seasonal transitions. We service all makes and models, including older systems common in Tahoe Park and College Greens neighborhoods.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive system inspection
Step 2: Cleaning and component testing
Step 3: Pressure adjustment and reprogramming
Step 4: Detailed report with recommendations
benefits: Prevents costly winter pipe damage
Ensures proper summer performance
Extends equipment lifespan by years
Peace of mind during temperature extremes

faq.question: How often should my system be serviced?
faq.answer: We recommend twice yearly - before summer watering season and before first frost.

faq.question: Do you service commercial properties?
faq.answer: Yes, we maintain systems for HOAs throughout Elk Grove and office complexes in the Central Business District.

faq.question: What if I have a smart controller?
faq.answer: We update all weather settings and check WiFi connectivity during service visits.

reviews.name: Meredith K.
reviews.text: Horizon transformed our East Sacramento cottage garden with a precision drip system that handles the clay soil beautifully. They suggested adding extra emitters for our roses that always struggled in summer heat. Two seasons later, everything thrives while using half the water of our old sprinklers.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: When a leak was flooding our Land Park driveway during 108°F temps, Horizon came within hours. They not only fixed the broken valve but reconfigured our zones to prevent future issues. Their knowledge of older neighborhood systems is unmatched.

reviews.name: Lisa T.
reviews.text: Our new smart controller automatically adjusts for Elk Grove's watering days and saved us $80 last month. The tech walked us through every app feature until we were comfortable. No more coming home to sprinklers running in the rain!

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Building a new home in Natomas, we needed irrigation that could handle the heavy soil. Horizon designed zones with proper drainage and used commercial-grade parts. Two summers in with zero issues - even during that brutal heat wave last September.

reviews.name: Sarah P.
reviews.text: As snowbirds, we rely on Horizon to winterize our Folsom home. They take photos of the blow-out process and store our controller inside. Came home this spring to a perfectly functioning system programmed for current restrictions.

reviews.name: David L.
reviews.text: Fixed our erratic sprinklers in Midtown that kept tripping the breaker. Turned out to be faulty wiring from the 1980s installation. They rerouted everything neatly and now all zones work perfectly. Appreciate their honest approach - no upselling.

reviews.title: Sacramento Irrigation Customer Reviews
reviews.description: See why Sacramento homeowners trust Horizon Irrigation for their landscape watering needs.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Irrigation provides top-quality irrigation solutions for Elk Grove's thriving master-planned communities. With hot, dry summers often exceeding 100°F, efficient irrigation is essential to maintain lush, healthy landscapes. Our services include smart sprinkler systems, drip irrigation, and water-efficient designs tailored to newer homes (1990s-2020s). We help homeowners conserve water while keeping lawns and gardens vibrant in this fast-growing Sacramento suburb.
serviceAreas.localContext: Elk Grove is a rapidly expanding city with modern planned neighborhoods and a focus on sustainable living.

serviceAreas.content: Roseville's well-planned communities (1990s-2000s) and scorching summer temperatures make professional irrigation a must. Horizon Irrigation specializes in custom systems for Roseville homes, ensuring water efficiency in this dry climate. From smart controllers to drought-resistant landscaping, we help residents maintain beautiful outdoor spaces without wasting resources. Our local expertise ensures your system is optimized for Roseville's unique conditions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Roseville combines modern suburban living with easy access to Sacramento and the Sierra foothills.

serviceAreas.content: Folsom's newer planned communities require irrigation systems that can handle extreme summer heat while conserving precious water. Horizon Irrigation designs and installs high-efficiency systems perfect for Folsom's climate, including smart technology that adjusts to weather patterns. Whether you're near Lake Natoma or in one of Folsom's growing neighborhoods, we'll create a system that keeps your landscape thriving through the hottest months.
serviceAreas.localContext: Folsom offers a mix of modern development and outdoor recreation along the American River.

serviceAreas.content: Rocklin's growing communities need irrigation solutions that balance water conservation with landscape health. Horizon Irrigation serves Rocklin homeowners with custom systems designed for hot, dry conditions. We install weather-based controllers and high-efficiency nozzles to protect your investment in your home's curb appeal. Our local knowledge ensures your irrigation system meets Rocklin's specific needs and any water use regulations.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rocklin is a family-friendly city with excellent schools and expanding residential areas.

serviceAreas.content: El Dorado Hills' upscale homes and challenging terrain demand specialized irrigation solutions. Horizon Irrigation designs systems that account for the area's elevation changes and hot summers. We focus on water-efficient technologies that maintain beautiful landscapes while respecting the region's limited water resources. From smart zone control to drip irrigation for native plantings, we help El Dorado Hills residents create sustainable outdoor spaces.
serviceAreas.localContext: El Dorado Hills offers luxury living with rolling hills and views of Folsom Lake.

serviceAreas.content: Rancho Cordova's mix of established and newer neighborhoods requires flexible irrigation solutions. Horizon Irrigation serves this diverse area with systems adapted to both older properties and modern developments. We address the challenges of 100°F+ summers with water-saving technologies and proper system maintenance. Our team understands Rancho Cordova's unique microclimates and designs accordingly for optimal performance.
serviceAreas.localContext: Rancho Cordova is a strategically located community between Sacramento and Folsom.

serviceAreas.content: Lincoln's rapid growth and hot climate make efficient irrigation essential for new homeowners. Horizon Irrigation specializes in systems for Lincoln's expanding communities, focusing on water conservation during scorching summers. We install smart controllers that adjust to weather conditions and high-efficiency sprinklers that reduce runoff. Whether you're in a new development or an established neighborhood, we'll design a system that meets Lincoln's unique needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Lincoln is one of California's fastest-growing cities with master-planned communities.

serviceAreas.content: Galt's agricultural heritage and newer residential areas both benefit from Horizon Irrigation's expertise. We design systems that handle extreme summer heat while conserving water - crucial in this semi-rural community. Whether you have a small urban lot or larger property, we create customized solutions that work with Galt's climate and soil conditions. Our maintenance services keep systems running efficiently year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Galt blends small-town charm with modern growth south of Sacramento.

serviceAreas.content: Citrus Heights' mature landscapes need irrigation systems that deliver both reliability and water efficiency. Horizon Irrigation updates older systems with modern water-saving technologies perfect for the area's hot summers. We specialize in retrofitting existing setups with smart controllers, efficient nozzles, and proper zoning to reduce waste. Our local team understands Citrus Heights' specific irrigation challenges and delivers tailored solutions.
serviceAreas.localContext: Citrus Heights is an established suburb with a mix of older and updated properties.

We help homeowners balance beautiful landscapes with water conservation in this established community.
